title: Camera System Health
exclusions: Does not certify video retention legal holds, perform pixel-level video analytics, or replace the VMS/NVR native health console; carrier WAN or building LAN faults outside camera or recorder control need separate network operations workflows unless those signals are already ingested elsewhere.
evidence: Retain the scheduled search or alert output listing `camera_id`, `location`, `connection_status`, `recording_status`, and `last_frame`, the Splunk job identifier, and a VMS/NVR screenshot or export for the same interval showing matching device state.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab VMS feed, mark one camera offline or set `recording_status=failed` while others stay `recording` with `connection_status=connected`; within the scheduled five-minute window the saved search returns that `camera_id` with the expected degraded fields and the analyst records the Splunk job SID next to the VMS alarm detail.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During a thirty-minute soak, every ingested row shows `connection_status=connected` and `recording_status=recording` for all cameras with no maintenance flags; the alert fires zero times even though event volume on `index=physical` is steady, proving healthy telemetry does not page.

dataSources: Video management system logs carrying **camera status** and **recording status** normalized to `index=physical` with `sourcetype="vms:camera_status"` and extracted fields **`connection_status`**, **`recording_status`**, **`camera_id`**, **`location`**, and **`last_frame`**. Typical paths: NVR/VMS **syslog** into Splunk Connect for Syslog ([Splunkbase app 4740](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)) or a Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` TCP/syslog stanza; optional HTTPS **`POST`** to Splunk HEC **`/services/collector/event`** when the VMS exposes JSON status APIs polled by a scripted or modular input.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="vms:camera_status"
| where recording_status!="recording" OR connection_status!="connected"
| table camera_id, location, connection_status, recording_status, last_frame
description: Detects data-center surveillance cameras that stop sending heartbeats or stop recording to the Video Management System by correlating connection and recording-state fields—so aisles, entries, and cages are not unknowingly unwatched.
value: Blind spots in camera coverage weaken after-action reviews for physical incidents, visitor escorts, and evidence for regulators or customers who expect continuous monitoring of sensitive colocation and white-space areas.
implementation: Normalize VMS/NVR syslog or API camera-health events onto `index=physical` as `vms:camera_status`, validate `connection_status` and `recording_status` literals against the vendor UI, schedule the catalog SPL every five minutes with suppression per `camera_id`, and tune thresholds or lookups for maintenance and standby modes before paging physical security.
visualization: Heatmap or status grid of `camera_id` × (`connection_status`, `recording_status`); sortable Table of offline or non-recording cameras with `location` and `last_frame`; Single value KPI for percentage of cameras in `recording`; timechart of degraded-count trends for executive summaries.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable — rely on raw `index=physical` `sourcetype="vms:camera_status"` searches because no accelerated CIM node is assumed for generic VMS telemetry; revisit only if you FIELDALIAS camera attributes into a curated data model after volume review.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Forward data to Splunk Enterprise
Splunk Documentation — Create and manage alerts
Splunk Documentation — REST endpoint POST stream/collector/event (HEC)

knownFalsePositives: (1) **Planned VMS maintenance window** — The Video Management System may mark cameras `not_recording` during controlled upgrades; suppress when **`maintenance_window=true`** on events or throttle using a lookup tied to change tickets. (2) **Camera reboot after firmware or lens cleaning** — Short `connection_status=disconnected` bursts during controlled power cycles resemble outages; require duration thresholds (for example two consecutive **`*/5`** intervals) before paging. (3) **Temporary storage rollover on the NVR** — When the Network Video Recorder pauses ingest to reindex volumes, **`recording_status`** may flash non-recording though the imager is online; correlate with **`storage_pool`** warnings if exported or widen suppression during vendor-documented housekeeping. (4) **Bandwidth-saving standby modes on perimeter cameras** — Some streams idle overnight yet remain compliant; differentiate using **`stream_profile`** or schedule-based exemptions so overnight eco modes do not flood on-call.
equipmentModels: Network Video Recorder (NVR) hosting recorded camera streams for data-center halls
Video Management System head-end exporting syslog or HTTPS status to Splunk
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Confirm the deployment pattern described in **App**: **NVR/VMS syslog or API**—either syslog from the recorder/VMS head-end or scheduled HTTPS calls to vendor status endpoints that forward into Splunk.
• Ingest must land on **`index=physical`** with **`sourcetype="vms:camera_status"`** and stable extractions for **`connection_status`**, **`recording_status`**, **`camera_id`**, **`location`**, and **`last_frame`**; spot-check Search in a `-15m@m` window before enabling alerts.
• Prefer Splunk Connect for Syslog ([Splunkbase 4740](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)) or an `inputs.conf` listener on a Heavy Forwarder with TLS and a dedicated sourcetype override; for API bridges, document the HTTPS path, poll interval (for example **`*/5 * * * *`**), and any auth token vaulting—never paste secrets into props.
• RBAC: scope a role such as **`physical_security_analyst`** to **`index=physical`** only and grant **`schedule_search`** to the service account that owns the scheduled detection.
• Clock skew: require NTP on cameras/NVR/VMS so **`last_frame`** and `_time` comparisons match operator consoles.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Map each **`camera_id`** to **`location`** strings (hall, cage, mantrap) consistently with CAD floor plans used by guards.
• If multiple sites share one index, add **`site`** or **`campus`** at ingest via **`transforms.conf`** routing from source host or subnet.
• For API feeds, confirm the modular or scripted input writes the same field names the catalog SPL expects, or clone the search with your aliased fields.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save the catalog SPL as a report, then clone to a scheduled alert with **`cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *`**, **`dispatch.earliest_time=-5m`**, **`dispatch.latest_time=now`**, and suppression keyed on **`camera_id`** for at least one interval to limit duplicate notifications during flapping links.

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="vms:camera_status"
| where recording_status!="recording" OR connection_status!="connected"
| table camera_id, location, connection_status, recording_status, last_frame
```

Understanding this SPL
• **`index=physical`** and **`sourcetype="vms:camera_status"`** must match your props/TAs; rename only if your build standard differs—keep Data sources and SPL aligned.
• **`where recording_status!="recording" OR connection_status!="connected"`** surfaces cameras that lost heartbeats or stopped persisting segments—tune literals to your vendor vocabulary (`online`/`offline`, `ok`/`fail`) via a lookup if needed.
• **`table`** projects operator columns for ticketing and dispatch to guards or smart-hands vendors.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick two cameras in the VMS/NVR UI—one healthy, one deliberately disabled in a maintenance window—and compare connection and recording badges to Splunk rows for the same interval; export a CSV from the VMS if auditors require side-by-side evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize and troubleshoot
Publish a dashboard with a status matrix by **`location`**, a table of failing **`camera_id`** values, and a single-value panel for percentage recording; wire alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty) per your physical-security runbook. If noise spikes, check syslog queue depth on the forwarder, HEC ACK failures on **`/services/collector/event`**, or mass maintenance flags from the VMS that should drive lookup-based suppressions.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
